message:
fix(http): extend request timeout to cover response body streaming

Previously, the timeout only applied to the send_parts future (getting
status code and headers). Once headers arrived, response.text() would read
the body stream with no time limit, meaning a slow-drip server could stall
the agent indefinitely.

- Add deadline: Option<Instant> field to Response struct, computed as
  now + duration at send time when a timeout is configured
- In Response::text(), wrap the body-reading try_fold in a select() race
  against the remaining deadline, returning a timeout error if exceeded
- All test Response constructions receive deadline: None

This closes a potential hang where connect+headers complete within timeout
but body streaming takes forever.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
